title: toddalia willdenowii
intro: Toddalia willdenowii, also known as the East Indian pepper or Toddy-pod, is a tropical plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Toddalia willdenowii include relief from pain, fever reduction, and treatment of digestive issues, as well as its potential use in managing diabetes. Herbal preparations of Toddalia willdenowii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be made from the plant's leaves, roots, or berries, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
